subject: AoA route: organizer renewal-reminder system (channel + billing half)
body:
Engelbart — Toby ratified a new initiative 2026-06-21 and asked me to loop you in NOW per the AoA Gate. Routing the two AoA-owned halves to you.

THE IDEA: An organizer renewal-reminder system. Recurring calendar events are bounded (12-mo RRULE per BTC->RRULE / CALBEAF-200), so they dead-end. Before a series expires we proactively message the owning organizer on a cadence (T-30 / T-14 / T-1 day). They can (a) log in and edit, or (b) one-click 'yes, extend' and we push the series out 3/6/12 months for them. Prototype in Boston, appID=1, 10 organizers (Toby first -> friendlies -> 10). Eventually a PAID TIER with a portal so organizers manage renewals without logging into the calendar.

THE SPLIT:
- MINE (Quinn / app-side): renewal-detection engine, per-series state machine, signed-magic-link auth, the extend mutation (Calendar-Invariants-gated), organizer identity. I am drafting the design-on-paper doc now.
- YOURS (AoA): (1) external interaction CHANNEL — Toby's working default is email-with-signed-link for the pilot, but he left FB Messenger / SMS / portal-first open; (2) the BILLABLE/paid-tier layer + metered provider (SendGrid/Twilio/Meta) + how a paid organizer connects via portal vs login. HDTS portal (Firebase Auth + Stripe) is a precedent to mine.

ASK: Start designing the interaction + billing half in parallel. Key open question for you: cheapest authenticated channel for a 10-org pilot that does NOT prematurely commit us to a paid/metered platform. I will share my app-side doc when drafted so the token/action contract lines up with your channel. Toby is HITM on both.
